data.body:
TARGETS (derived; to be confirmed). Story = schema-coverage guard test + per-command output-contract docs. Depends on T1-T4 (all schemas + --output commands exist).

NEW TEST:
- tests/interfaces/test_schema_coverage.py — enumerate EVERY structured (--output json) output across CLI/library/MCP; assert (a) each validates against a registered schema AND (b) no structured output lacks a schema (coverage enforced, not aspirational). Drive via a registry mapping command->schema name.

REGISTRY:
- src/rebar/schemas/__init__.py — a COMMANDS/coverage map (command/interface -> schema) as the single source the guard test consumes.

DOCS:
- README.md — document the canonical --output/-o flag + per-command output contract; link schemas.
- CLAUDE.md (tool guide) — MCP tool set + outputSchema note; flag change.
- docs/event-schema.md — link the output schemas (note: schemas are replay-derived state, distinct from events).
- docs/architecture.md — interface/output-contract section.
- Decision doc: schema source-of-truth (hand-authored JSON Schema files vs pydantic-generated) + drift guard if both coexist (the MCP pydantic models vs schema files — cross-interface test pins both).
